narrative.headline: 2020 Honda HR-V: electrical and fuel-system issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 79 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 23%, followed closely by the fuel system and gasoline-related reports (18% each). Power train complaints (15%) include the only crash in the data. No fires, injuries, or deaths are reported across any area.

issues.sample: Faulty design by honda for sunroof drain tubes. Clogged drain tubes cause water to intrude cabin and get fuse panel wet and inoperperable. Nothing about drain tube maintenace in manual. Honda denied repair stating because of rain. No easy access to clean drain tubes on vehicle. C
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 18 reports (23%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe electrical faults such as a soaked fuse panel from clogged sunroof drain tubes.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 14 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports reference a fuel-system recall and an illuminated check-engine light.

issues.title: Gasoline
issues.blurb: Also 14 reports (18%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. These overlap with the fuel-system recall and a check-engine warning light.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 12 reports (15%) and the only crash in the data (1), with no fires or injuries. Owners describe sudden loss of power when merging or accelerating.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 11 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports again point to loss of power while driving.
